ks1.objective: Run with a bib in your hand, throw it on the call, and find the matching colour corner.

lks2.objective: Move around the area, swap bibs with others, and on 'Bib Toss!' race to the corner matching the bib in your hand — scanning, choice, communication.
successCriteria: Quick reaction to the 'Bib Toss!' call

space.description: Square area. Place blue cones in two corners and yellow cones in the other two corners.

value.name: Inclusivity
value.text: Every child feels welcome and included — every session designed so everyone can take part, have fun and succeed. Adapt to suit each child, helping them build confidence, make friends and feel valued.

steps: Each child is given a blue or yellow bib to hold in their hand.
Run around the area swapping bibs with others.
On the coach's command 'Bib Toss!', children throw their bibs into the air.
Each child catches or picks up a bib, then runs to the matching colour corner.
Last team to all return completes a fun challenge set by the coach.
progressions: Vary movement when swapping (hop, skip, side-step).
Add footballs — same task but with a ball at the feet.
Quietly move corner cones while children aren't looking — tests awareness and scanning.
Children must put the bib ON before running to the corner.
Max number of children allowed at each cone — they must communicate to balance.
questions: How did you know the corners might have moved?
How did you help someone who didn't notice?
What did you do when there were 'too many' at one corner?

step.space: Stretch the area to challenge scanning and longer sprints
step.task: Complete a specific task (5 toe taps) before running to the corner
step.equipment: Use additional colour bibs and corners — three or four teams
step.people: Pair children up — they must end up at the same corner together

base.showmanship: Use positive reinforcement throughout the session to keep children engaged. Praise scanning, awareness, and the small moments of children helping one another find the right corner.
